description_long: This house features a rectangular, block-like structure with a distinct two-tone exterior: the upper half is clad in reddish-brown brickwork, while the lower half is finished in a textured, weathered plaster. The front facade displays two windows with dark wooden frames, one larger window on the upper story and a smaller one below, both set into the brick and plaster sections. The other sides of the house are plain, lacking windows or doors, and continue the two-tone material division. The flat roof is slightly weathered, with a subtle color gradient suggesting exposure to the elements. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on simple, functional design. The structure is reminiscent of early 20th-century urban residential buildings found in dense city neighborhoods.
description: A two-story, rectangular house with a flat roof, brick upper facade, and plastered lower section, featuring two front-facing windows with dark wooden frames.
description_view_0: This side shows the plain brick and plaster exterior with no windows or doors, highlighting the two-tone material split.
description_view_1: Similar to view 0, this side is also windowless and doorless, emphasizing the brick and plaster textures under direct light.
description_view_2: The front view displays two windows with dark wooden frames, one on each story, set into the brick and plaster facade.
description_view_3: This side mirrors view 0, with a plain brick and plaster finish and no visible openings.
attribute_entryway_design: No visible entryway; main facade features only windows with dark wooden frames and no decorative elements.
